summary: PR 2412 linked itkirbycutterbeam as Matching and also added or improved item decomp work in Ness yoyo and Ice Climbers string/link-style item files. The bot report records 8 new matches, including itkirbycutterbeam .data, several yoyo/string physics callbacks, and several near-matches in unmatched yoyo/string functions.

key_files.role: Changed itkirbycutterbeam.c from NonMatching to Matching in MatchingFor.
Cleaned includes and added ItemStateTable it_803F6798, corresponding to bot-reported .data match.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itkirbycutterbeam.c include cleanup and ItemStateTable it_803F6798[]
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports itkirbycutterbeam .data +56 bytes 0.00%->100.00%

key_files.role: Implemented multiple Ness yoyo functions and matching support code around ItemLink chains, matrices, cleanup, collision, and scale/position updates.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itNessyoyo_UnkMotion1_Phys, itNessyoyo_UnkMotion2_Phys, itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim, it_802BF030, it_802BF180, it_802BF800, it_802BF900, it_802BFEC4, it_802C0010
text_corpus: bot reports yoyo new matches and improvements

key_files.role: Header declarations adjusted for Ness yoyo work.
Implemented multiple Ice Climbers string functions using ItemLink traversal, matrix-derived positions, cleanup, and action-range checks.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_802C28DC, fn_802C29E8, fn_802C2AF4, it_802C2CA8, it_802C2DB0, it_802C32D4, itClimbersstring_UnkMotion3_Anim
text_corpus: bot reports climbersstring new matches and improvements

key_files.role: Added prototypes and itCharItems.h include for climbers string functions and types.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itclimbersstring.h adds itCharItems.h include and prototypes

key_files.role: Refined itClimbersStringAttributes layout by splitting padding and naming float fields x8 and x14.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itClimbersStringAttributes pad_00[0x18] split into pad_00[0x8], f32 x8, pad_0C[0x8], f32 x14

what_changed: configure.py now treats melee/it/items/itkirbycutterbeam.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
itkirbycutterbeam.c gained ItemStateTable it_803F6798 with anim/phys/coll callbacks and removed several unused includes.
itclimbersstring.c added matrix-position helper callbacks, ItemLink chain adjustment functions, pickup/cleanup logic, and owner action-range cleanup checks.
itclimbersstring.h added declarations for newly implemented climbers string functions and included it/itCharItems.h for itClimbersStringAttributes.
itCharItems.h exposed two float fields in itClimbersStringAttributes at offsets +0x08 and +0x14.
itnessyoyo.c added several yoyo physics/animation/collision functions and matrix/ItemLink manipulation routines, while adding ftlib, itlinkhookshot, mpcoll, and math_ppc includes.
itnessyoyo.h was changed, but the loaded diff is truncated before the exact header edits.

smart_moves.text: The PR did not only flip configure.py; it also added the itkirbycutterbeam state table/data needed for the bot-reported .data match.
Repeated rope/chain logic was expressed as natural while loops over ItemLink prev/next links rather than as copied blocks.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: it_802C2CA8 and it_802C2DB0 while(prev != NULL)

smart_moves.text: Several functions recover world or local positions through PSMTXIdentity, HSD_JObjSetupMatrix, PSMTXConcat, then Vec3 extraction, a repeated pattern across yoyo and climbers string callbacks.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For chained item effects such as yoyo or climbers string, natural linked-list loops over ItemLink prev/next can express repeated segment propagation and cleanup clearly while still supporting matching progress.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: it_802C2CA8, it_802C2DB0, it_802BF180, cleanup loops

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function body reveals attribute offsets, refining the owning attributes struct can remove opaque padding and allow direct field use in matched item code.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itClimbersStringAttributes adds f32 x8 and x14
diff_excerpt: climbers string functions use attrs->x8 and attrs->x14

decomp_lessons.lesson: A code-linking PR may include small data-table recovery when that table is tied to the linked object and is reported as matching, but broader data ownership conclusions should not be inferred from this slice alone.

naming_conventions.name: address-style functions
naming_conventions.description: Unproven functions retain names such as it_802C2CA8, it_802BF800, fn_802C28DC rather than speculative semantic names.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: newly implemented climbersstring and yoyo functions retain address-style names

naming_conventions.name: short local role names
naming_conventions.description: Clear local roles use short names such as ip, fp, attrs, link, link1, link2, cur, prev, pos, dir, owner, and jobj.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itclimbersstring.c and itnessyoyo.c local variable declarations

naming_conventions.name: offset-based struct fields
naming_conventions.description: Recovered but not semantically named attributes remain offset-style x8 and x14, avoiding unsupported semantic naming.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itClimbersStringAttributes f32 x8 and f32 x14

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK retained in itClimbersstring_UnkMotion3_Anim after the return.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: Loaded evidence does not include author explanation or objdiff detail for this stack tactic beyond bot score improvements.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Explicit pad locals are used near matrix and vector temporaries, e.g. f32 pad[1], pad_top, pad_mid, pad_bottom.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: Global standards require verifier evidence for stack-shape tactics; the bot report supplies matching percentages but not local rationale for each pad.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: State table recovery in itkirbycutterbeam.c was paired with configure.py marking the object Matching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: The slice supports the data table change, but not broader data-section policy.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Uses HSD_JObjGetChild/GetRotationX/SetRotationX and HSD_JObjSetupMatrix/CopyMtx/SetMtxDirty instead of raw expanded jobj access in the visible yoyo code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: No review comment confirms this was intentional cleanup; evidence is from the diff shape.

accepted_candidate_records.title: ItemLink chain item code can often be represented as natural prev/next loops
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2412, climbers string and Ness yoyo functions implement segment propagation and cleanup as while/for loops over ItemLink prev/next pointers, aligning with the natural-loops standard and producing reported matches or near-matches.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: it_802C2CA8, it_802C2DB0, it_802BF180, cleanup loops
text_corpus: bot reports matches/improvements in these units

accepted_candidate_records.title: Matrix-derived ItemLink anchor positions recur in yoyo/string item callbacks
accepted_candidate_records.text: Several callbacks initialize a matrix, setup a JObj matrix, concatenate, then copy translation components into Vec3 before calling chain update helpers. This pattern appears in both climbers string and Ness yoyo code in the PR.

source_update_candidates.title: itkirbycutterbeam became Matching in PR 2412
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2412 changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itkirbycutterbeam.c is Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching), with bot-reported itkirbycutterbeam .data at 100%.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact for object matching status; curator should verify against current source/history before promoting.

source_update_candidates.title: itClimbersStringAttributes has float fields at +0x08 and +0x14 in PR 2412
source_update_candidates.text: The PR split padding in itClimbersStringAttributes to expose f32 x8 and f32 x14, which are used by climbers string chain update functions.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible struct layout path fact; field semantics remain unnamed in the PR and should not be over-interpreted.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not treat PAD_STACK or pad locals as self-justifying from a bot score alone
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2412 contains PAD_STACK and several pad locals while the bot report shows matches/improvements, but the slice lacks per-tactic justification. This supports retaining the existing standard that stack tactics need narrow verifier evidence rather than creating a new exception.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: f32 pad locals in yoyo/climbersstring functions
source_update_candidates.reason: Curator may use this as supporting example for an existing standard, not as a new accepted exception.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all yoyo/climbersstring score improvements were accepted as final matches; the bot lists several as improvements under unmatched items.
Do not infer semantic meanings for itClimbersStringAttributes x8/x14 or address-style functions from this PR alone.
Do not promote the PAD_STACK or pad locals as generally accepted style; the slice lacks explicit objdiff rationale for those tactics.
Do not claim human reviewer approval details; no human review comments are loaded.
